input.prompt: I need to convert this entire repository to Central Package Management. The solution is at advanced-multi-complexity/Enterprise.slnx. There are some complications: package versions defined as MSBuild properties, conditional package references for multi-targeting, and a shared Common.props that adds a package to all projects. Inline MSBuild property values and use the highest version for any conflicts.

data.content:
I have the full picture. Let me summarize the complexity:

- **MSBuild properties**: `$(BlobsVersion)=12.24.0`, `$(HostingVersion)=8.0.1` in Directory.Build.props
- **Version conflicts**: `System.Text.Json` (10.0.1/9.0.0/8.0.5), `Azure.Storage.Blobs` (12.24.0/12.19.0)
- **Conditional refs**: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson` with different versions per TFM in Api.csproj
- **Shared props**: `Common.props` adds `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging` to all projects

Let me implement CPM now.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="12.24.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="8.0.11" Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0'" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="6.0.36" Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net6.0'" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

arguments.old_str:

    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="$(BlobsVersion)" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net6.0'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="6.0.36" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="8.0.11" />

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me analyze:

**MSBuild Properties to inline:**
- `$(BlobsVersion)` = `12.24.0` (defined in Directory.Build.props, used in Api.csproj)
- `$(HostingVersion)` = `8.0.1` (defined in Directory.Build.props, used in Core.csproj)

**All packages across all projects:**

From Common.props (imported by all via Directory.Build.props):
- `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging` Version="9.0.0"

Api.csproj:
- `System.Text.Json` Version="10.0.1"


- `Azure.Storage.Blobs` Version="12.24.0"
- `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson` with conditional versions for net6.0 (6.0.36) and net8.0 (8.0.11)

Core.csproj:
- `System.Text.Json` Version="9.0.0"
- `Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting` Version="8.0.1"

Legacy.csproj is next on the list.

Looking at the remaining projects, I'm seeing `System.Text.Json` Version="8.0.5" and `Azure.Storage.Blobs` Version="12.19.0" in Legacy.csproj, then Tests.csproj has `xunit` Version="2.9.3" and `System.Text.Json` Version="10.0.1", and Web.csproj includes `System.Text.Json` Version="10.0.1" plus `OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting` Version="1.15.0". Now I'm identifying the version conflicts across all projects—`System.Text.Json` ranges from 8.0.5 to 10.0.1 so I'll use the highest at 10.0.1, and `Azure.Storage.Blobs` goes between 12.19.0 and 12.24.0 so I'll take 12.24.0.

For the conditional package reference in Api.csproj with `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson` having different versions for net6.0 and net8.0, I need to figure out how to handle this in Central Package Management—the approach involves either using multiple `PackageVersion` entries with conditions in Directory.Packages.props or using `VersionOverride` in the project file for the conditional versions. the conditions are evaluated per target framework during the restore process. I'll use conditional `<PackageVersion>` entries since that's the standard approach for this scenario. Now I'm setting up the Directory.Packages.props file with the centralized package management enabled and defining the package versions...

Writing package configuration...

Actually, I'm realizing there's a conflict here—you can't have two `<PackageVersion>` entries for the same package because the last one wins in MSBuild, so the conditional version would override the unconditional one when the target framework matches.

The right way to handle this is to use mutually exclusive conditions: one `<ItemGroup>` for net6.0 with version 6.0.36, and another `<ItemGroup>` for everything else with version 8.0.11.

Actually, the cleaner approach is to put both package versions in a single `<ItemGroup>` with conditions on each item itself—that way net6.0 gets 6.0.36 and net8.0 gets 8.0.11.

Now I need to update the project files by removing the `Version` attribute from all `<PackageReference>` elements while preserving any existing `Condition` attributes, then clean up any other version-related attributes.

I should also create a new `Directory.Packages.props` file to centralize the package versions, remove the version properties from `Directory.Build.props`, strip the `Version` from `Common.props`, and update the `Api.csproj` to remove inline version specifications.

Now I need to apply the same changes across the remaining project files—`Core.csproj`, `Legacy.csproj`, `Tests.csproj`, and `Web.csproj`—removing the `Version` attributes from their PackageReferences as well.

  </PropertyGroup>

    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="8.0.11" Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0'" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="6.0.36" Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net6.0'" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
